$description: Hindi/Devanagari typography overrides (ADR-080). These tokens override the default Latin typography when content is in Hindi. Devanagari script has different optical properties: wider glyphs, shirorekha (headline), higher x-height equivalent. These differences require specific typographic treatment, not just font substitution.

reading.$description: Hindi reading. 20px (vs 18px Latin) — 11% larger due to Devanagari optical size. 1.9 line height (vs 1.8 Latin) — shirorekha needs more vertical clearance. Noto Serif for reading warmth.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Devanagari aksharas are optically wider and taller than Latin glyphs. Without size adjustment, Hindi text feels smaller than equivalent English text. The 11% increase achieves optical parity.

ui.$description: Hindi UI text. Noto Sans (not Serif) for chrome — cleaner at small sizes. 15px (vs 14px Latin) for optical matching.

line-length.$description: Optimal Hindi line length in aksharas (40-50 range). Devanagari glyphs occupy more horizontal space — fewer characters per comfortable line.

drop-capitals.$description: Drop capitals are OMITTED for Devanagari. No equivalent in Hindi typographic tradition. The shirorekha makes drop capitals visually awkward.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Devanagari's shirorekha (headline connecting bar) conflicts with drop-capital spacing. Hindi literary tradition does not use drop capitals.

quotation-marks.$description: Hindi quotation conventions. Hindi typically uses the same quotation marks as English (double quotes), but some publishers use guillemets. SRF/YSS publications should be checked for their convention.

primary.$description: Left/right double quotation marks. Standard in most Hindi publishing.

secondary.$description: Left/right single quotation marks. Quotes within quotes.

terminology.$description: Hindi is PRIMARY for many Yogananda terms, not derivative. Many terms originated in Hindi/Sanskrit. The vocabulary bridge maps colloquial Hindi states to Yogananda's formal vocabulary — the reverse direction from English. (ADR-130, DES-059)
terminology.review: Requires YSS editorial review for cultural fidelity (PRO-043)
